# READY FOR FRESH VALIDATION (v2 — repair) — br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.1

**Agent:** MaroonSpire. Addresses @PeachOtter's two blocking cross-review findings. **Not self-closing.**

## Finding 1 — topology surfaced in the lobby UI (deferment removed)
- `create_room` already accepts topology (default local); **NetworkSystem `network:roomCreated`
  now carries `topology`** (`static/js/systems/NetworkSystem.js`).
- **LobbyUI renders a topology badge** above the room code (`#topology-badge`): "📺 Local play" /
  "🌍 Remote play" / "🔀 Mixed play", colour-keyed via `data-topology`. New public
  `LobbyUI.setTopology()` (coerces unknown→local); consumed from the `network:roomCreated` handler
  and from `GameHost` after room creation. Styles in `static/css/host.css` (`.topology-badge`).
- **E2E coverage added** — `tests/e2e/room-topology.spec.ts`:
  - host lobby shows the Local badge by default (visible, `data-topology=local`, text "Local");
  - badge updates correctly for remote + mixed and coerces garbage→local.
  - Visual evidence captured (opt-in `TOPOLOGY_EVIDENCE_DIR`): local (cyan) + remote (pink) +
    mixed (amber) badge screenshots.
- The full Local/Remote **chooser screen** (doc §4.0, only meaningful once Remote exists) remains
  scoped to **br-76ia** (blocked by .1 and .2) — but the *surfacing* the bead asks for is now done.

## Finding 2 — mixed/remote role semantics no longer collapse
Roles are now **capabilities** (a list), not one collapsed label. `session_vocabulary.participant_roles()`
+ `primary_role()`; `game_joined` emits both `roles` (list) and `role` (primary):
- local  player → `['controller']`; **LOCAL GUARD**: `can_render` is ignored → never a viewer.
- local  host   → `['host']` (renders shared world, not a driver).
- remote player → `['controller','viewer']` (driver-viewer); remote host → `['host','controller','viewer']` (host-driver).
- mixed  player → `['controller']` (Local-style HUD-only) or `['controller','viewer']` when `can_render`.
- spectator → `['viewer']` (remote/mixed) / `[]` (local, shared screen).
`join_game` accepts an optional `can_render` hint (honoured only in mixed; forced off in local).

## Evidence (all run after the repair)
- `python -m unittest server.test_socket_routing` → **14 passed** (incl. mixed, Local-guard, and a
  direct `participant_roles`/`primary_role` capability-combination test).
- `npm test` (unit+integration+server) → **16 + 14 passed**.
- `npm run build` → OK.
- `npx playwright test full-game game-modes join-flow console-errors room-topology` → **16 passed**.
- Contract proofs printed (disjoint topology/ruleset/role value sets; capability table above;
  LOCAL ARCHITECTURE GUARD asserted: local phone stays controller-only even with can_render=true).

## Edge cases covered
default→local; explicit remote/mixed stored+echoed; invalid topology→local; topology⟂ruleset
(mode=derby leaves topology=local, distinct keys); reclaim preserves/defaults topology; local
controller-only + can_render guard; remote driver-viewer; mixed HUD-only vs driver-viewer; spectator;
host-driver/host-renderer combos; badge default + remote/mixed + garbage-coercion in the UI.

Reserved (held): server/app.py, server/test_socket_routing.py, server/session_vocabulary.py,
static/js/systems/NetworkSystem.js, static/js/GameHost.js, static/js/engine/sessionVocabulary.js,
static/js/ui/LobbyUI.js, static/css/host.css, tests/e2e/room-topology.spec.ts.
(LobbyUI.js / host.css were pre-dirty from @PeachOtter's work — my edits are additive; RoomCodeOverlayUI.js
changes in the tree are not mine and are preserved.)

title: Usability improvements: learn-to-play and general UX polish
description:
Grab-bag of usability work to help new players learn and reduce friction, complementing the existing first-run tutorial.

acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Required skill gate: run `.claude/skills/game-model-prep/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/vehicle-model-validation/SKILL.md`; completion note must include a Stage H PASS report for this specific vehicle.
- Prep manifest: `model-prep.json` records normalization, rig/wheels, collider/CoM, physics metrics, balance/archetype, debris manifest, material/color QA, light QA, and final Stage H status.
- Visual/material proof: `/car-viewer` diagnostics plus host-style screenshots cover default color and at least two player colors from front/rear/side/top or equivalent, proving `paint` recolors while tyres/glass/lights stay readable.
- Physics/balance proof: headless sim metrics cover no wheel orbit, scale/origin/forward-axis, collider fit, low enough CoM, top speed, acceleration, turn radius, wall-graze, rollover, settle, archetype band, and win-rate spread.
- Debris proof: declared wheels/parts detach or the model records an explicit scoped exception; debris inherits velocity, cleans up or hands off to the shared debris manager, and respects budgets.
- Catalog/security proof: selectable vehicle id is catalog-only, sanitized, no embedded cameras/lights are accepted, texture/material budgets are recorded, and unknown client URLs/classes/styles are rejected.
- Manual rollout guard: if this is one of the first two pilot vehicles, it feeds `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.39`; otherwise it remains blocked by that gate until cdilga personally approves broad rollout.

title: JJ adapter: glTF loader + VehicleFactory gltf path + rubric.json + stat->param transfer fn
description: Implement the game-model-prep adapter for JJ: ResourceLoader.loadModel (three GLTFLoader, npm), VehicleFactory visual.mode:'gltf' branch (keep userData.isWheel sync + primitive fallback), normalize (recenter/scale to length 4, stamp paint/tyre/glass/metal/light), concrete static/assets/vehicles/rubric.json from the template, and the stats->Rapier transfer function. Verified: no loader exists today; CoM works via collider.setMassProperties; no top-speed cap; side-friction unused. docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit CI: `npx vitest run tests/unit/gltf-loader.test.js tests/unit/vehicle-factory-gltf.test.js` covers GLTFLoader wiring, primitive fallback preservation, wheel `userData.isWheel` sync, normalized scale/recenter, and stats-to-Rapier transfer parameters.
- Normal integration CI: `npx vitest run tests/integration/vehicle-gltf-render.test.js tests/integration/vehicle-physics.test.ts` proves selectable glTF vehicles render through VehicleFactory and remain compatible with physics setup.
- Artifact: `static/assets/vehicles/rubric.json` or equivalent catalog adapter exists, includes material roles needed by Stage H, and rejects unknown client URLs/classes/styles.
- Scope guard: this adapter must not mark a model ship-ready; it only enables the later render, material/light, physics, balance, and manual gates.

Seed from frontend/car-viewer. CI-safe WebGL flags. Outputs diffable PNGs. docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit/browser CI: render-harness tests load normalized glTF vehicles with host shaders/lights, deterministic camera presets, color variants, roof number overlays, and safe error rendering.
- Normal E2E: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/car-viewer.spec.ts tests/e2e/console-errors.spec.ts` or a focused render-harness spec captures nonblank front/rear/side/top/three-quarter screenshots for at least one debug car and one catalog car.
- Host parity proof: harness uses the same renderer/material/light conventions as the host path or documents exact remaining differences.
- Evidence artifact: exported screenshot bundle and diagnostics include vehicle id, source file, material counts, texture counts, lights/emissive state, paint color, camera preset, and build id.

title: Promote /car-viewer debug editor into the vehicle QA tool
description:
Make the existing `frontend/car-viewer` debug editor a first-class vehicle QA and authoring support tool in the model-import plan. This bead is about preserving and hardening the useful editor that already exists, then wiring it into the evidence flow for per-model work.

Background:
The current `/car-viewer` tool already loads `/debug-cars/manifest.json`, classifies materials into body/wheel/glass/light/other groups, supports recolor modes, shows headlights/tail lights/underglow/name tag/number behavior, and includes rig/suspension preview controls. That is valuable planning infrastructure and should not be treated as a throwaway debug page.

Scope:
- Keep `/car-viewer` or its successor as a documented local QA/editor surface for vehicle imports.
- Ensure it can load both `static/debug-cars/*` sources and normalized selectable vehicle catalog entries, so agents can compare raw imports against production-ready output.
- Preserve controls for material classification, naive-vs-normalized recolor comparison, paint color swatches, wheel/rig preview, suspension bob/steer preview, headlights/tail lights, underglow, name tag, number/decal readability, wireframe, and multi-angle inspection.
- Add or document screenshot/export evidence from the tool: material role summary, texture/material counts, active vehicle id, color, light toggle state, and captured views.
- Make clear that this is a local QA/debug tool, not a player-facing car picker. The actual picker still uses sanitized catalog ids and appearance payloads.
- Use the editor findings to feed the vehicle-model-validation Stage H report rather than replacing the formal gate.

Acceptance criteria:
- The model-import plan names `/car-viewer` (or successor path) as the manual/debug QA tool and requires its evidence for per-model visual review.
- The tool can inspect a raw debug car and a normalized selectable vehicle entry without hand-editing source code.
- Screenshots or exported diagnostics show recolor separation, wheel/rig preview, lights/underglow/name/number state, and material classification results.
- The workflow documents how an agent uses the tool before closing a model bead and how failures become authoring fixes rather than ad-hoc runtime hacks.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit CI: `npx vitest run tests/unit/car-viewer-diagnostics.test.js tests/unit/safe-rendering.test.js` covers diagnostics schema, material classification, recolor separation metadata, and literal rendering of hostile model names/errors.
- Normal E2E: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/car-viewer.spec.ts tests/e2e/console-errors.spec.ts` proves raw debug-car and normalized catalog modes load, canvas is nonblank, screenshots/diagnostics hooks work, and no console errors occur.
- Evidence bundle: exported diagnostics include active vehicle id, raw/normalized source, material roles, texture/material counts, paint color, light/underglow/name/number state, and multi-angle screenshots.
- Scope guard: `/car-viewer` is a local QA tool and never a player-facing trust boundary; production picker continues to use sanitized catalog ids.

title: Vehicle material, texture, and light acceptance gate
description:
Add an explicit material/texture/light acceptance gate to the vehicle import pipeline so imported models do not merely load - they are readable, recolorable, lit correctly, and production-usable in the actual game.

Background:
The model-prep plan already requires Stage H validation, but recent work showed unresolved issues around texturing/coloring and vehicle lights. The gate needs to make these failures first-class: no single-color blobs, no baked-light texture problems that block recolor, no missing paint slot, no wheels/glass/tyres recolored accidentally, no embedded GLB lights/cameras, and no headlights/tail lights that point the wrong way or detach from the rig.

Scope:
- Define and enforce normalized material roles: `paint`, `tyre`, `glass`, `metal/trim`, `light`, and any allowed decals/number surfaces.
- Require production recolor behavior: changing player color affects only paint/approved decal surfaces while tyres remain neutral, glass stays readable, lights remain emissive, and numbers/name markers stay legible.
- Validate texture/material budgets: texture sizes, material count, draw-call implications, no surprise giant baked textures, no embedded cameras, no embedded runtime lights, and no per-car PointLights in the selectable GLB path.
- Validate lighting/readability in actual host-style rendering, not only a neutral model viewer: front/rear/side/top screenshots, at least two paint colors, lights on/off, underglow where applicable, and a dark/neon track lighting case.
- Verify headlights/tail lights are oriented correctly, ride with suspension/body transforms, do not float when the rig animates, and remain visible without becoming blinding.
- Feed both deterministic checks and visual review: glTF inspection output, `/car-viewer` diagnostics, headless render screenshots, and the vehicle-model-validation Stage H report.
- Failure mode must be authoring/normalization fixes, not runtime heuristic hacks that hide bad source assets.

Acceptance criteria:
- The prep-manifest runner records material-role mapping, texture/material counts, recolor evidence, and light evidence for each model.
- A model cannot pass if paint recolor also tints tyres/glass/lights, if `paint` resolves to nothing, if light materials are missing/wrongly oriented, or if embedded lights/cameras sneak into selectable vehicles.
- Evidence includes `/car-viewer` diagnostics plus actual host/render-harness screenshots for default color and at least two player colors.
- The vehicle-model-validation Stage H completion note must call out material role pass/fail, texture budget pass/fail, and light orientation/readability pass/fail separately from physics/balance.
- At least the first two imported cars receive manual human playtest review before the broad model rollout continues.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit CI: `npx vitest run tests/unit/vehicle-material-gate.test.js tests/unit/vehicle-light-gate.test.js` rejects missing paint, paint bleeding into tyres/glass/lights, embedded cameras/lights, over-budget textures/materials, wrong light orientation, and unresolved material roles.
- Normal E2E/visual QA: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/car-viewer.spec.ts` captures default color plus at least two player colors across front/rear/side/top and a dark/neon lighting preset.
- Stage H report: vehicle-model-validation output must list material role pass/fail, texture budget pass/fail, recolor pass/fail, light orientation/readability pass/fail, and host-style render pass/fail separately from physics/balance.
- Manual gate linkage: first two pilot imports cannot unlock broad rollout until `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.39` records cdilga's playtest approval.

title: Manual human playtest gate after first two vehicle imports
description:
Manual coordinator gate: the first two imported/normalized vehicle models must be personally playtested by cdilga before the broader vehicle rollout continues. Agents must not close this bead based only on automated tests, screenshots, or their own judgment.

Default first-two pair for graph enforcement:
- `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.10` hatchback-sports
- `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.11` sedan-sports

If the human coordinator chooses a different first-two pair before work starts, update this bead dependencies and description to match that explicit choice. Until then, the graph treats those two starter cars as the pilot imports.

Scope of the human playtest:
- Review each of the two cars in the car selection/customization path and the actual host game render.
- Try at least one race and one derby context if both are available at that point, or explicitly record which mode was unavailable.
- Check the subjective parts automation can miss: does the car look good, does recolor preserve readable materials, do lights/readability feel right, does the silhouette match the intended archetype, does the vehicle feel usable rather than merely passing physics metrics.
- Confirm the vehicle-model-validation Stage H evidence includes material/texture/light results, `/car-viewer` evidence, host screenshots, and relevant test/check output.

Acceptance criteria:
- This bead can close only after cdilga has actually playtested the first two cars at that time and posted explicit approval in Beads or Agent Mail.
- The approval must identify the two vehicle ids reviewed and state PASS for broad rollout, or list blockers/fixes.
- If either car fails the human review, this gate stays open and the failed model bead(s) get reopened or receive follow-up beads.
- No remaining per-model import/balance beads should be treated as ready for broad rollout until this manual gate is closed.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Manual only: cdilga must personally playtest the two pilot vehicle ids at that time; agents cannot close this from automated checks, screenshots, or proxy judgment.
- Required human note in Beads or Agent Mail: identifies the two vehicle ids, confirms race and derby tried or states which mode was unavailable, and says either PASS for broad rollout or lists blockers/fixes.
- Required technical evidence before human review: Stage H reports for both pilot cars include material/texture/light results, `/car-viewer` diagnostics, host/render screenshots, and relevant unit/E2E/check output.
- Failure behavior: if either car fails, this gate stays open and the failed per-model bead receives blockers or follow-up beads; no later per-model rollout bead should be treated as unblocked.

comments.author: OliveFox
comments.text: Human follow-up 2026-06-29: cdilga confirmed this first-two-car playtest gate is intentionally a blocker. Do not bypass it with automation, screenshots, or agent judgment; broad model rollout remains blocked until the human playtest note explicitly passes the pilot cars.

title: Deterministic CV/geometry gates (coverage, symmetry, pivot/orbit, deltaE, ground, number)
description: Hard CI gates from model+renders: AABB vs length, ground contact, wheel perpendicular-offset/no-orbit (seeded by inspect-wheel-pivots.mjs), collider coverage, CoM below center, paint deltaE, left/right symmetry, number legibility, debris count/velocity. docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit CI: deterministic CV/geometry tests cover silhouette coverage, left/right symmetry, wheel pivot/orbit, wheel contact/ground offset, color deltaE for paint variants, roof number position/legibility, collider-fit overlay, and material role masks.
- Regression proof: bad fixtures fail for wheel orbit, missing paint, paint leaking to tyres/glass/lights, wrong ground offset, unreadable number, and asymmetric/cropped render.
- Determinism proof: same model/build/camera preset produces stable measurements within documented tolerances.
- Stage H integration: prep-manifest and vehicle-model-validation consume these gates and record pass/fail separately from physics/balance.

title: Vision-LLM rubric judge + calibration
description: Vendor-agnostic vision-LLM judge over the multi-angle renders returning forced JSON (rubric.template.json). Calibrate against a human-labeled starter-set sample before gating. Layers 1-2 own measurements; this owns 'looks right/on-brand'. docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Artifact: a calibrated vision-LLM rubric or equivalent visual review prompt/checklist covers recognizability, material separation, color readability, number readability, wheel rig sanity, light readability, scale, camera views, and obvious visual defects.
- Calibration proof: known-good and known-bad fixtures produce expected pass/fail or human-review-needed verdicts with reasons.
- Evidence guard: LLM rubric verdict cannot replace deterministic gates; it is an additional review layer recorded in Stage H.
- Privacy/repro guard: screenshots sent to any model are generated artifacts only, with no secrets/tokens/user data, and the verdict is saved with prompt/model/version where available.

title: Headless physics sim metrics (top speed, accel, turn radius, settle, rollover, wall-graze)
description: Headless PhysicsSystem loop measuring objective per-model metrics via vc.currentVehicleSpeed() etc. Feeds archetype target bands and the balance gate. docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal integration CI: headless physics tests measure top speed, acceleration, braking, turn radius, settle time, rollover tendency, wall-graze behavior, bad-landing stability, and reset/recovery behavior for representative vehicles.
- Regression fixtures: wrong scale, too-high center of mass, bad collider fit, excessive rollover, wheel orbit, and wall snag cases fail with diagnostics.
- Evidence artifact: physics metrics JSON includes vehicle id, collider dimensions, mass/CoM, suspension/wheel params, tuning hash, seed, and pass/fail thresholds.
- Stage H integration: prep-manifest and per-model beads consume these metrics before balance or manual playtest claims.

title: Balance framework: stat schema + archetype map + transfer fn + Monte-Carlo win-rate gate
description: Mario-Kart-style net-fair balance: fine-grained int stats (Speed/Accel/Weight/Handling/Traction + hidden boost reusing stunt charge), shared point budget with inverse coupling, silhouette->archetype map, stats->Rapier transfer function, Monte-Carlo win-rate spread gate (winRateTolerance). docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Artifact: checked-in stat schema, archetype map, shared point budget, inverse coupling rules, and stats-to-Rapier transfer function define Speed/Accel/Weight/Handling/Traction and any hidden boost/stunt fields.
- Normal unit/integration CI: balance tests cover transfer-function output ranges, archetype bands, deterministic Monte Carlo race/derby simulations, win-rate spread tolerance, and no hidden model advantage outside declared stats.
- Tuning evidence: each starter archetype has a metrics report showing performance, handling, stability, and win-rate spread under the same seed/script suite.
- Gameplay guard: balance changes must not bypass material/visual Stage H, physics stability gates, or the first-two-car human playtest gate.

title: Destructible debris system (detach parts, inherit velocity, TTL, pooling) in DamageSystem
description: Generic detachable-debris on damage:destroyed (both modes): reparent declared debris-* parts, dynamic body + cheap collider, transfer velocity + blast impulse, TTL fade, object pool with cap. Per-model beads only declare which parts shed. docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit/integration CI: debris tests cover per-model debris manifest loading, wheel/panel detach, velocity inheritance, impulse direction, pooling, TTL for this generic system, cleanup, and no NaN/Inf state.
- Visual evidence: render/harness screenshots or captures show detached wheels and panels leaving the wreck, with debris material roles and scale sane.
- Performance guard: body/render counts stay within the generic pool cap for 4/8/16 vehicle destruction stress; no per-frame logging.
- Scope handoff: this bead provides the generic destructible debris source; persistent round-level hazard priority/budget is owned by `br-captain-call-architecture-hardening-woq.6`.

title: Prep-manifest stage runner (model-prep.json, gates A-H)
description: Stage runner that advances a model through A-H, records results in model-prep.json (schema in skill), resumable/auditable. Gates per stage. docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
The prep-manifest runner is complete only if it makes the model QA gates auditable and hard to skip.

- `model-prep.json` records stage results for normalization, rigging, collider/CoM, balance, debris, color/material QA, light QA, and final Stage H validation.
- The runner stores or links the evidence needed by `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.38`: material role map, texture/material counts, recolor screenshots, `/car-viewer` diagnostics, host/render-harness screenshots, and light orientation/readability checks.
- A model cannot advance to Stage H if material roles are unresolved, paint recolor leaks into tyres/glass/lights, headlights/tail lights fail orientation/attachment/readability checks, or embedded cameras/lights are present in selectable vehicles.
- Output is resumable and clear enough that another agent can inspect why a model is blocked without rerunning every stage.
- The runner does not bypass the manual first-two-car playtest gate; it prepares evidence, while `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.39` controls broad rollout after the first two pilot cars.

title: Remote Play mode: separated players join a shared host-rendered room
description:
New Remote Play mode so people in different locations play together. Builds on the Local single-screen model but lets every participant join from their own computer/device into the same room.

Proposed architecture (host-authoritative):
- One participant is the HOST: runs the authoritative physics sim and the canonical render, exactly like the big screen does today.
- Everyone else gets their OWN browser view of the gameplay (a VIEWER render driven by state synced from the host) PLUS controller input for their car. This sidesteps running N independent authoritative sims and keeps everyone in sync.
- Any number of people can play; non-driving spectators are viewers-only.
- Host migration / 'host leaves' handling: at minimum, detect host loss and end/park the match gracefully; ideally promote another participant.

Open questions to resolve during design (capture decisions in the bead before coding):
- State sync granularity & rate (full snapshots vs deltas) for the viewer render.
- Latency handling for the local player's own car (client-side prediction vs pure mirror).
- Bandwidth ceiling for 'any number' of viewers.

Depends on the game-mode architecture bead. Coordinate with rejoinable-rooms and deeplink-invite.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal server/client CI: tests prove Remote room topology, host authoritative state, viewer bindings, controller bindings, spectators, and room cleanup without breaking Local mode.
- Normal E2E: Playwright multi-context flow creates a Remote room, joins at least two separated participants, proves each gets viewer+controller or spectator behavior as intended, and verifies host authoritative state drives viewers.
- Sync/perf evidence: diagnostics record snapshot/delta cadence, payload size, reconnect behavior, viewer degradation path, and no uncontrolled per-frame event spam.
- Role guard: viewer-only sockets cannot drive, controller sockets drive only their bound seat, and Local controller phones remain HUD-only.
- Open-design closure: state sync granularity, local prediction vs mirror, bandwidth ceiling, and host-loss behavior are documented or split into follow-up beads before close.

title: Rejoinable rooms: rooms persist across matches and survive disconnects
description:
Make game rooms durable so they can be rejoined after a match ends and after a dropped connection, in both Local and Remote modes.

Scope:
- A room outlives a single match: after results, players stay in the room and can start another match without re-creating/re-sharing a code.
- Reconnect: if a player's socket drops, they can rejoin and reclaim their car/seat (by stable player token, not socket id) within a grace window.
- Server holds room + per-player state (name, car, customization, score) keyed by a persistent token, not the transient connection.
- Clean up genuinely abandoned rooms after a timeout.
- Tests: drop a player mid-match and rejoin to same seat; finish a match and start a new one in the same room.

Foundational for deeplink invites (a link should drop you into a live, rejoinable room).
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal server CI: room/seat tests cover room persistence across results/rematch, stable player/seat tokens, mid-match disconnect, grace-window reconnect, expired reconnect, host loss, room TTL, and abandoned-room cleanup.
- Normal E2E: Playwright drops a player mid-match and rejoins the same seat; finishes a match and starts a rematch in the same room without re-sharing a code.
- Identity proof: name, color, number, vehicle selection, score/progression, and controller role survive valid reconnect and do not merge with a different device lacking a valid token.
- Security guard: guessed/reused/stale reconnect ids cannot hijack another seat, and stale controller lease input is rejected.

title: Deeplink invites: one-tap shareable link that joins a specific room instantly
description:
Add shareable invite URLs so someone can join a room without manually typing a room code, while preserving the explicit role-intent flow from the join-route bead.

Scope:
- Generate links encoding the room plus enough non-secret context for route entry, for example `/join/<ROOMCODE>` or `?room=CODE&via=invite`; do not encode authority tokens in public copied links.
- Opening a copied/shared link routes into the join chooser from `br-captain-call-architecture-hardening-woq.11` rather than silently deciding the role. The player chooses whether this device is their screen/viewer that will pair a phone controller, controller-only because another screen is visible, or spectator where supported.
- Host/lobby QR remains separate from copied links: scanning it implies the host screen is visible and routes directly to controller-only/HUD-only.
- Pair QR remains separate from join links: scanning it binds a phone controller to an existing viewer/screen seat.
- Provide Copy invite/share affordances in host lobby and, for Remote/mixed where allowed, participant/viewer UI.
- Gracefully handle room ended, invalid/expired code, incompatible topology, revoked/locked access where explicitly supported, and pair-token failures with clear recovery actions.
- Do not treat high occupancy as a routine capacity-limit failure. Copied invites should keep attempting to join/bind in 32/60/100-player situations, then rely on camera/render degradation, ruleset late-join policy, diagnostics, or real technical failure handling if the system cannot keep up.
- Pairs with rejoinable rooms so a link works mid-session, not only at room creation.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit/browser CI: invite route tests cover copied invite link, host QR, pair QR, reconnect link, invalid room, expired room, room ended, incompatible topology, high-occupancy/capacity-stress room, and the copied-link role chooser.
- Normal E2E: opening a copied Remote/mixed invite joins the intended room and shows the explicit choice between second-screen/viewer-with-phone-pair, controller-only because the game screen is visible, and spectator where supported.
- Second-screen E2E: choosing the screen/viewer option from a copied invite starts the viewer flow and displays a pair QR/code; scanning that pair QR with a phone binds the phone as controller for the same seat.
- Host QR regression: scanning the host lobby QR does not show the chooser and goes directly to controller-only/HUD-only without initializing the world renderer.
- Capacity-stress regression: high-occupancy invite tests prove the route does not show a planned capacity-limit rejection; it keeps attempting the join/bind path and surfaces best-effort degradation/diagnostics only when pressure is real.
- UX proof: copied invite, host join QR, and pair QR are visually distinct and have clear labels/states; invalid/expired/ended links show recovery actions without blank pages or silent fallback to another room, while high-occupancy links use capacity-stress language rather than arbitrary-limit copy.
- Architecture guard: Local controller paths stay HUD-only; Remote viewer paths initialize the viewer renderer only after explicit intent and degrade gracefully.

title: Align in-game UI with the front-page neon aesthetic
description:
Bring every in-game surface into line with the front-page (landing) aesthetic. Source of truth is the live landing page; the captured tokens and direction live in docs/design-brief.md.

Scope (audit + restyle, surface by surface):
- Phone controller (player.css): join screen, waiting room, in-game HUD, menus, weapon/fire controls -> glows, glass panels, pill buttons, neon palette, focus states.
- Host screen (host.css): lobby, scoreboard, overlays, results.
- Adopt the shared token set (colors, radii, spacing, glows) rather than ad-hoc hex values currently scattered in host.css/player.css.
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion.
- Test: rebuild (npm run build) and visually verify with playwright headed/screenshots; would each screen sit comfortably next to the landing page?

Note: phrased as 'consistent with whatever the front page IS' — re-check against the live landing page, not a frozen snapshot.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Build check: `npm run build` passes after token/style changes.
- Normal E2E/visual smoke: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/landing-page.spec.ts tests/e2e/join-flow.spec.ts tests/e2e/game-flow.spec.ts tests/e2e/console-errors.spec.ts` proves host/player/lobby/results surfaces load without console errors and remain usable.
- Screenshot evidence: before/after or final screenshots cover phone join/controller/waiting HUD, host lobby, scoreboard/overlays/results, and at least one reduced-motion state.
- Design guard: reuse shared tokens from the live landing page/design brief; avoid ad-hoc scattered colors, broken focus states, unreadable glow, or text overflow on mobile.

title: Own-car identification: make it obvious which car is yours
description:
Players consistently lose track of their own car. Make own-car identity unmistakable on both the host render and (for Remote) each viewer render.

Scope:
- Persistent marker over/around the local player's car (arrow, ring, or floating name tag) that stays readable at distance and in crowds.
- Use the player's chosen color/customization for the marker.
- Brief 'this is you' highlight on (re)spawn and on rejoin.
- Readable in both race and derby modes; works with camera modes.
- Test: with 4 cars, the local player's car is identifiable at a glance in screenshots.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit/browser-light CI: `npx vitest run tests/unit/own-car-marker.test.js` or equivalent covers marker selection, color/number/name inputs, distance scaling, and spawn/rejoin highlight timing.
- Normal E2E/visual smoke: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/camera-modes.spec.ts tests/e2e/game-flow.spec.ts` proves a 4-car Local race and derby scene make the controlled car identifiable at a glance without covering HUD/name tags.
- Remote guard: each Remote viewer identifies its own car independently; Local phones remain HUD-only controllers.
- Evidence artifact: screenshots for race, derby, respawn/rejoin highlight, and a crowded/high-N or simulated crowd case.
notes: Release manager blocked: dirty tree with 49 changed files prevents safe scoping of this bead's validated changes. Awaiting coordinator guidance on file ownership across beads or alternative release strategy.
close_reason: Completed and independently validated: NobleEagle reran own-car-marker vitest 6/6, npm build, and Playwright camera/game flow 11 passed/1 skipped; inspected race, derby, chase-focus, respawn, and rejoin screenshots; Local controller-only guard and Remote architecture scope verified.

comments.author: SageBasin
comments.text: Fresh validation BLOCKED by PurpleWaterfall. Unit own-car-marker tests passed 5/5 and npm run build passed, but E2E camera-modes/game-flow passed 9/11 with two blocking failures: chase mode only shows 2/4 markers vs expected >=3, and derby markers overlap HUD/camera controls, violating the no-HUD-covering acceptance criterion. Respawn/rejoin pulses and Local host-only bootstrap look sound. Do not close; fix safe-area/marker rect/culling or test expectation as appropriate, rerun unit/build/E2E, then request fresh validation.

title: Car models: proper 3D vehicle models with variety
description:
Replace placeholder car geometry with proper 3D car models, with enough variety to support per-player distinction and customization.

Scope:
- Source/build a small set of low-poly arcade car models (bundled, not CDN).
- Load via the existing resource/vehicle factory pattern; keep physics chassis decoupled from the visual mesh.
- Performant for multiplayer (instancing / shared geometry where possible).
- Models tint/skin cleanly for customization (see car-customisation bead).
- Test: models render on host and viewer; no perf regression with 4+ cars.
acceptance_criteria: Before closing this bead, use `.claude/skills/vehicle-model-validation/SKILL.md` and include its PASS report in the completion note. Required evidence includes: normalized material separation (`paint` recolors while `tyre` stays neutral), wheel rig/state preview, scale/origin/forward-axis check, per-model collider fit, screenshots/visual evidence from the actual viewer or game path, and relevant asset/test/check output. The model import is not done if the skill reports blockers, even if gameplay mechanics still function.

title: Car customisation: pick color/skin (and model) from the lobby
description:
Let each player customize their car so identity is personal and persistent.

Scope:
- Lobby/waiting-room customization UI (neon-styled): color, and if available model/skin choice; live preview (reuse the existing car-preview).
- Customization travels with the player token so it persists across matches and rejoins (ties into rejoinable-rooms).
- Drives own-car-identification marker color and other players' readability.
- Sensible defaults / auto-assign distinct colors when players don't choose.
- Test: choose a color, start match, confirm the car uses it on host + viewer; rejoin keeps it.
acceptance_criteria:
Before closing this bead, use `.claude/skills/vehicle-model-validation/SKILL.md` for any selectable vehicle preview/model path changed by the customization UI. Required evidence includes catalog-only vehicle IDs, sanitized appearance payload (`vehicleId`, `color`, `number`, `skinId` or current equivalent), screenshots proving recolor/number readability in the picker and host render, and relevant E2E/check output. The selection/customization work is not done if model validation reports blockers for any selectable vehicle.
- Rollout guard: color/number customization may ship before broad model rollout, but selectable vehicle/model choices must stay catalog-only and cannot expose unapproved models until `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.39` records cdilga pilot approval. Vehicle choice must not create hidden handling advantage without balance evidence.

title: Pickup models: proper 3D models for weapon/item pickups
description:
Give weapon/item pickups proper 3D models instead of placeholders, matching the arcade/neon look.

Scope:
- Bundled low-poly models (or stylized glowing primitives) for each pickup type used in derby.
- Glow/float treatment consistent with the neon aesthetic (drop-shadow/emissive).
- Clear at a glance what each pickup is.
- Performant spawn/despawn.
- Test: pickups render and animate on host + viewer; collecting still works.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Blocking tool gate: `/weapon-lab` from `br-weapon-test-lab-zas` is available for local pickup/weapon visual verification before this bead closes.
- Normal unit/browser-light CI: `npx vitest run tests/unit/pickup-models.test.js tests/unit/weapon-definitions.test.js` proves every pickup type maps to a bundled asset or stylized primitive with no CDN/runtime URL dependency.
- Normal E2E/visual smoke: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/weapon-lab.spec.ts tests/e2e/game-modes.spec.ts` proves pickups render/animate on host/lab, collecting still works, and no console errors occur.
- Performance/readability guard: pickup assets are low-poly/capped/emissive enough for derby crowd stress and remain distinguishable at a glance in the neon style.
- Dependency guard: `/weapon-lab` from `br-weapon-test-lab-zas` is a blocking prerequisite, not an optional relation; pickup art cannot close until the lab can verify pickup visuals and behavior through the production path.

title: Lo-fi retro design language ("Skip Bin Arcade")

title: G2: Perf spike - calibrate adaptive-quality ladder for the grade
description: Ship ALL grade effects; do NOT cut by default. Spike measures per-effect GPU cost and per-tier fps on worst-case supported host hardware (old laptop / weak desktop driving the shared screen) and, separately, weak Remote viewer devices where Remote mode renders locally. Use the measurements to CALIBRATE the adaptive ladder (1.9): hardware-detection heuristic, dynamic resolution scaling (target native, drop internal res gracefully to hold fps), and the order in which heavier effects (vertex-snap/affine 1.8, then dither/grain) shed. Also locks the tone-mapping decision, including whether to skip ACES. Output = cost table + degrade-ladder thresholds + tone-map decision. Resolves docs/design/00 open Qs 4&5. Coordinate with `br-captain-call-architecture-hardening-woq.2` render backend.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Benchmark artifact: checked-in table or report records per-effect GPU/frame cost for the committed grade stack on at least one worst-case supported host profile and one normal host profile; Remote weak-device measurements are separate and must not gate Local host play.
- Ladder decision: adaptive-quality thresholds specify hardware detection inputs, dynamic-resolution steps, effect-shedding order, minimum acceptable readability, and recovery/hysteresis behavior after pressure drops.
- Visual proof: screenshots or captured frames compare native/full-grade, degraded ladder tiers, and fallback path for lobby, race, derby, and high-player host scenes.
- Tone-map proof: decision log records ACES/skip-ACES outcome with visual comparison and why it matches the Skip Bin Arcade grade without crushing player readability.
- Test/profiling proof: automated smoke or documented manual harness records no blank canvas, no shader errors, and stable backend metadata while toggling grade tiers.
- Architecture guard: Local phones/controllers never become the host-renderer performance target; host rendering budgets apply to the shared-screen host, while Remote viewers degrade independently.
notes: Release manager blocked: dirty tree with 49 changed files prevents safe scoping of this bead's validated changes. Awaiting coordinator guidance on file ownership across beads or alternative release strategy.

description: DECISION 2026-06-29: NO explicit rubber-banding system (no speed manipulation, no Blue-Shell-style position-targeting catch-up). A LITTLE catch-up to keep it interesting is fine, but achieve "keep them together" through SOFT/IMPLICIT mechanics: (1) arena/track design that funnels cars back together (chokepoints, loops, re-merging shortcuts, short tracks); (2) the shrinking derby arena (already exists) - forces proximity without touching speed; (3) make the LEADER a target (weapons/attention converge) so being ahead carries risk and the pack naturally compresses; (4) short rounds so gaps never grow large. At most a subtle position-weighted item luck - keep it barely noticeable. Implementation lives in 7.1 + level-design (coord ...3xv.13 curated maps, ...3xv.9 comeback tuning, map-authoring epic).

title: Sound design engineering pipeline and validation
description:
Create a real sound-engineering pipeline for Joystick Jammers instead of adding ad hoc samples or one-off oscillator snippets.

Problem this closes:
The repo already has audio infrastructure and useful experiments: `AudioSystem.js`, `audioManager.js`, procedural `EngineSynth`, music/SFX volume controls, hardcoded bundled samples, synthesized missile/explosion helpers, and design notes around jsfxr/sfxr, Web Audio, lo-fi grade, weapon sound problems, boost feel, and audio budgets. What is missing is a durable pipeline that tells agents how to research, author, audition, validate, bundle, route, and regression-test new sounds.

Owner clarification captured 2026-06-29:
- Add a deep research task on the pipeline of sound design, including libraries and methods for generating sounds.
- Create a reusable skill for Claude and Codex that encodes real sound design principles and gives the LLM qualitative feedback loops so it can reason about sounds it generated or modified.
- Ensure the project audio infrastructure can play whichever source strategy wins: bundled samples, procedural synth, MIDI-generated/rendered sounds, or a hybrid.
- New sounds need an audio generation, wiring, and validation pipeline, not taste-only commits.

Design intent:
Sound is a first-class feel channel for around-the-couch fun: speed, boost, wheelie, collision, weapon, pickup, result, lobby energy, and lo-fi world texture should all communicate state without muddying the room. The pipeline must support human taste/playtest gates while still giving agents objective artifacts they can inspect: waveforms, spectrograms, loudness/peak/crest factor, spectral centroid, transient envelope, duration/silence/clipping checks, event timing, voice counts, and A/B notes.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Child coverage: research, runtime infrastructure, local sound lab, reusable Claude/Codex skill, and new-sound acceptance gate each have their own complete Beads and acceptance criteria.
- Existing audio work is wired: lo-fi audio pass, boost/wheelie audio cues, weapon/pickup audio, and around-couch audio budgets cannot close with ad hoc sound changes that bypass the pipeline.
- Validation model: the pipeline records both objective audio checks and qualitative/human playtest gates; it never pretends metrics alone prove taste.
- Repo fit: the approach respects no-CDN runtime rules, browser audio unlock policy, Local host-vs-controller roles, Remote viewer graceful degradation, and high-player voice-budget constraints.
- Graph hygiene: `br dep cycles --blocking-only --json` returns zero active blocking cycles after dependency edits.

title: SND research game-audio generation methods and validation pipeline
description:
Run a deep current-state research pass on practical game sound-design pipelines for this project.

Scope:
- Research real game-audio design principles relevant to arcade racers and party games: transient/body/tail layering, impact sweetening, engine loops/synthesis, UI earcons, boost ramps, weapon identity, pickup readability, loudness hierarchy, masking, fatigue, repetition variation, ducking, voice limiting, and room-social readability.
- Compare implementation methods: bundled authored samples, Web Audio procedural synthesis, jsfxr/sfxr-style parameter synthesis, Tone.js-style musical/sequencer tooling, MIDI composition/rendering, generated audio from external tools/skills, and hybrid pipelines.
- Evaluate current browser/runtime constraints: Web Audio unlock, `OfflineAudioContext` testability, decoding/bundling formats, mobile/browser autoplay policy, latency, memory, voice count, and no-CDN/runtime dependency rules.
- Produce a decision matrix for which sound families should use sample, synth, MIDI/rendered, or hybrid sources: engines, boost/wheelie, collisions, weapons, pickups, UI, music/stingers, ambience/tape hiss, crowd/result moments.
- Identify candidate libraries/tools and their licensing/maintenance/runtime cost. Do not add dependencies during research; recommend only after checking current docs and repo fit.
- Decide what belongs in normal CI, opt-in audio QA, manual playtest, and human taste gates.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Research artifact: checked-in document summarizes current sources, links, candidate libraries/tools, licensing/dependency implications, and why each is or is not suitable for this repo.
- Decision matrix: every major sound family has a recommended source strategy: bundled sample, procedural synth, jsfxr/sfxr-style params, MIDI/rendered asset, hybrid, or defer.
- Validation plan: artifact defines objective metrics and qualitative review for each sound family, including what can be automated and what needs human ears/playtest.
- Runtime-fit proof: recommendations explicitly account for Web Audio unlock, `OfflineAudioContext`, no-CDN runtime, asset bundling, high-player voice budgets, Local host/controller roles, and Remote viewer degradation.
- Follow-up wiring: if a chosen library/dependency is recommended, create or update a specific implementation bead with tests and bundle-size/runtime-risk notes rather than silently adding it here.

title: SND audio runtime manifest and sample-synth-midi infrastructure
description:
Audit and harden the project runtime audio infrastructure so the final pipeline can support bundled samples, procedural synthesis, MIDI/rendered assets, or a hybrid.

Current starting point:
- `static/js/audioManager.js` hardcodes music and SFX asset paths, owns `AudioContext`, volume, ducking, sample decoding, synthesized missile/explosion helpers, and cached noise via `Math.random`.
- `static/js/systems/AudioSystem.js` maps game events to audio, but many weapon/pickup/checkpoint events reuse placeholders.
- `static/js/audio/EngineSynth.js` provides a procedural engine synth.
- `static/audio/music/**` and `static/audio/sfx/**` contain bundled mp3 assets.

Scope:
- Introduce a sound manifest/spec layer for every playable sound: id, family, source type, asset path or synth/MIDI params, bus, priority, voice policy, cooldown, variation, ducking, loop behavior, intended game events, and validation thresholds.
- Build or specify a playback adapter that can route samples, procedural Web Audio builders, and MIDI/rendered clips through the same buses and volume controls.
- Decide how MIDI is supported if selected: either pre-rendered/bundled outputs, a lightweight runtime player, or an authoring-only path. Do not strand the project with a MIDI plan that cannot play in production.
- Add seeded/deterministic options for generated noise and parameter variation where tests need repeatable output.
- Define bus/voice budgets for host, Remote viewer, and high-player scenes: master/music/sfx/engine/ui/weapon/ambience, priority stealing, cooldowns, and distance/importance rules where applicable.
- Preserve browser audio unlock UX and avoid per-frame logging.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Runtime audit artifact names current audio entry points, loaded assets, hardcoded mappings, placeholder mappings, unlock behavior, and gaps for sample/synth/MIDI/hybrid support.
- Manifest proof: tests or docs define the sound spec schema and include representative entries for engine, boost/wheelie, collision, missile, explosion, pickup, UI, and music/stinger sounds.
- Playback proof: unit/browser-light tests prove sample and procedural sound entries can be resolved and routed through the same volume/bus/cooldown/voice-budget layer; MIDI support is either implemented, pre-rendered, or explicitly authoring-only with no production ambiguity.
- Determinism proof: procedural noise/variation used in tests can be seeded or captured so `OfflineAudioContext` analysis is repeatable.
- Architecture guard: Local phones/controllers do not instantiate unnecessary world audio systems; host owns shared Local presentation, while Remote viewers degrade independently.
- No-regression proof: existing lobby/race/results music, engine synth, collision sounds, and audio unlock behavior still work after the abstraction.

title: SND local sound lab for audition tuning and evidence bundles
description:
Build a local sound lab/debug tool for authoring, auditioning, tuning, and exporting evidence for Joystick Jammers sounds.

Scope:
- Add a local/dev route or equivalent script surface such as `/sound-lab`, following the shared debug-lab pattern used by car viewer, weapon lab, and map tools.
- Load the production sound manifest and let developers audition every sound family without entering a live match.
- Provide controls for parameters that agents often need to tune: envelope, pitch, filter, distortion, noise color, modulation, variation seed, cooldown, bus, ducking, voice priority, loop points, volume, and source type where applicable.
- Render sounds through `OfflineAudioContext` where possible to generate waveform/spectrogram/features without relying only on real-time playback.
- Export an evidence bundle: sound spec JSON, source asset or synth params, rendered WAV/preview where legal and useful, waveform/spectrogram image, feature metrics, A/B comparison notes, and manual listening notes.
- Integrate with weapon/pickup/boost/wheelie use cases so gameplay sounds can be tested in isolation and, where useful, from a weapon-lab or scenario event.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal E2E or browser-light proof: `/sound-lab` or equivalent loads with no console errors, can audition at least sample, procedural synth, and one generated/parameterized sound spec, and exports diagnostics.
- Offline analysis proof: representative sounds render through `OfflineAudioContext` and produce waveform/spectrogram/features in machine-readable artifacts.
- Tuning proof: changing a parameter updates the sound spec, preview, and metrics without touching production code by hand.
- Integration proof: at least one weapon sound, one vehicle-feel sound, one UI sound, and one music/stinger entry can be auditioned from production manifest entries.
- Evidence bundle proof: exported bundle is sufficient for a fresh validator to inspect the sound without rerunning the full game.

title: SND create reusable sound-engineering skill for Claude and Codex
description:
Create a reusable sound-engineering skill for Claude and Codex that applies real sound-design principles while giving agents objective and qualitative feedback loops for sounds they cannot literally hear.

Scope:
- Create a project-scoped skill under `.claude/skills/` and make it discoverable for Codex as well, matching the pattern used by project model-validation skills.
- Keep the skill generic enough to reuse on other game/audio projects, but include a Joystick Jammers adapter section: Local/Remote roles, no-CDN runtime rule, sound manifest, sound lab, bundled assets, and required evidence before closing sound beads.
- Include a concise workflow: derive sound intent, choose source strategy, author/generate, render preview, analyze features, inspect visual/audio artifacts, run rubric, iterate, wire through manifest, validate in gameplay context, then attach evidence.
- Include sound-design principles: transient/body/tail, spectral space, loudness hierarchy, masking, repetition variation, loop points, engine/boost/impact/weapon/UI differences, fatigue, readability in a noisy room, and style matching to the lo-fi/arcade grade.
- Provide LLM feedback techniques: waveform and spectrogram images, feature metrics, A/B comparison tables, descriptive rubrics, failure signatures, and explicit uncertainty/human listening gates.
- Prefer reusable scripts or references for analysis rather than making every agent rewrite audio-analysis code.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Skill artifact: `.claude/skills/<sound-skill>/SKILL.md` exists with valid frontmatter, concise instructions, and project adapter notes; Codex discoverability/install instructions or symlink support are present where this repo expects them.
- Validation scripts/references: the skill includes or points to reusable scripts/references for rendering/analyzing audio evidence: waveform, spectrogram, duration, peak/RMS/LUFS-like loudness, crest factor, spectral centroid, silence/clipping, and transient envelope where practical.
- Qualitative rubric: skill tells agents how to produce a judgement packet the LLM can reason over and when to escalate to human listening/playtest because metrics are insufficient.
- `quick_validate.py` or equivalent skill validation passes, and at least one dry-run task demonstrates the skill can evaluate a sample/procedural sound artifact without relying on hidden context.
- Scope guard: the skill bans runtime CDN audio dependencies and requires project evidence before closing any new Joystick Jammers sound bead.

title: SND new-sound acceptance gate and existing audio bead wiring
description:
Define and enforce the acceptance gate for adding or changing any gameplay sound after the sound pipeline exists.

Scope:
- Update relevant audio beads and future new-sound work so they cannot close by merely adding an mp3 or oscillator function.
- Require each new/changed sound to have a manifest entry, intent statement, source strategy, validation thresholds, event wiring, bus/priority/voice policy, and evidence bundle.
- Define family-specific checks for engine, boost/wheelie, collision, weapon, pickup, UI, music/stinger, and ambience/tape-hiss sounds.
- Require gameplay-context proof for any sound that communicates state: e.g. boost/wheelie steering authority, missile launch danger, pickup confirmation, collision severity, or results/lobby energy.
- Preserve human taste gate for signature sounds, but make the human review packet concrete: A/B evidence, waveform/spectrogram, metrics, in-game capture, and playtest notes.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Gate doc or tests define the required checklist for every new/changed sound: intent, source, manifest, bus, voice policy, event wiring, objective metrics, lab evidence, gameplay proof, and human/manual gate when needed.
- Existing relevant beads are wired to the gate: lo-fi audio pass, boost audio ramp, wheelie sound cues, weapon/pickup sounds, and around-couch audio budgets mention or depend on this gate.
- Normal tests or lint checks catch missing manifest entries, broken asset paths, missing procedural builders, invalid bus/priority values, and absent validation metadata.
- E2E or lab evidence shows at least one sample sound and one procedural sound pass through the full gate.
- No taste-only closure: close notes for sound beads must include evidence bundles or a clear manual human listening gate, not just prose that it sounds better.

description: Create a durable, indexed user-flow database for Joystick Jammers covering Local couch play, Remote play, mixed/topology joins, reconnects, spectating, race/derby win conditions, error states, and missing expert-game-design edge cases. title: Local weapon test lab separate from the main game
description:
Build a standalone /weapon-lab that mirrors the existing /car-viewer paint/model debug editor: full-screen Three.js viewport, compact inspector panels, production code paths, deterministic reset/step hooks, visual overlays, parameter tuning, screenshots, and machine-readable diagnostics.

Background:
Weapons currently require a live room and normal match flow to test pickups, fire events, projectiles, deployables, buffs, hitscan, AOE, zones, continuous effects, damage, VFX, and HUD notifications. That slows iteration and hides bugs. The lab must exercise the real WeaponSystem and production track/arena data, not a mocked duplicate.

Scope:
- Add a dev route such as /weapon-lab with Vite/server wiring similar to host/player/car-viewer surfaces.
- Instantiate real WeaponSystem, with fake render/damage adapters only where needed to drive the production behavior under a local scenario.
- Load production tracks/arenas through TrackFactory so spawn areas, pickup zones, barriers, ramps, and surfaces match game behavior.
- Provide presets: pickup field, missile chase, mine arming, oil slick, rail gun, EMP blast, shield/boost, flamethrower cone, and crowd stress.
- Show overlays for spawn areas, pickup positions, projectile paths, mine trigger/explosion radii, hitscan lines, AOE radii, oil zones, flamethrower cones, target health, stun/buff timers, shield state, inventory, and emitted weapon events.
- Provide tunables for projectile speed/lifetime/tracking, mine arm delay/radius, buff duration/multipliers, hitscan range/aim tolerance, AOE radius/stun duration, zone radius/friction/lifetime, continuous cone/range/tick rate/damage, damage/falloff, progression weights, spawn interval, and max active pickups.
- Provide Reset, Step 1 frame, Step 1 sec, Play/Pause, Screenshot, Export JSON, Import JSON, and Run checks.
- Scenario JSON begins with schema jj.weaponLabScenario.v1 and records seed, trackId, preset, arenaConfigPatch, weaponOverrides, actors, and camera/overlay settings.
- Use GameRunContext clock/rng once available. No Math.random/performance.now patching as the final design.
- Expose window.__weaponLab with reset, step, screenshot, runChecks, and getDiagnostics for tests.
- Avoid per-frame console spam; use explicit event log and diagnostics export.

Acceptance criteria:
- A developer can select every current weapon type, tune uncertain parameters, run a preset, step deterministically, and export an evidence bundle.
- The lab exercises the same WeaponSystem behavior used by the game for spawn, pickup, fire, hit, buff/stun, projectile/effect lifetime, cleanup, and emitted events.
